design_philosophy.core_loop: Flip enemies onto their backs by hitting platforms beneath them, then kick them away before they recover. Manage POW block usage (3 activations per level) and time pressure as enemies gradually become more threatening.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure after flipping enemies—they will self-recover and resume threat if not kicked quickly. Progressive difficulty through enemy variety (Shellcreeper, Sidestepper, Fighter Fly, Slipice) and environmental hazards (icicles forming on platforms).
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Sewer maintenance plumber defending New York's underground against bizarre creature infestations. Cooperative or competitive arcade challenge with pattern recognition and reflex demands.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately subverts typical platformer enemy mechanics: cannot defeat enemies through jumping/stomping. Requires unconventional attack vector (hitting platform beneath them). POW block as consumable strategic resource (3-use limit) creates meaningful decision-making. Wraparound screen encourages spatial puzzle-solving. No jumping on enemies—only flipping and kicking—was intentional design decision that diverges from contemporary platformers.

difficulty.lives_system: Single life lost per enemy/fireball/icicle contact. Game ends when all lives depleted. Multiple lives provided at start (T2 data does not specify exact count).
difficulty.checkpointing: Phase-based progression with no mid-phase checkpoints. Completing all enemies in a phase advances to next. Bonus stages intersperse main phases.
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Arcade game with fixed difficulty curve. NES version maintains arcade difficulty. Player skill determines survival through pattern learning (enemy spawn timing, movement prediction) and execution (timing of platform hits and kicks).

progression.unlock_mechanism: Phases/levels unlock sequentially by eliminating all enemies in current phase. Bonus stages appear between main phases.
replayability_hooks: Score chasing—points awarded for enemy defeats and bonus coin collection
Multiple enemy types with distinct movement patterns encourage strategic adaptation
POW block resource management creates variable solutions per playthrough
Arcade pacing rewards speed and efficiency

world_building_techniques: Setting: New York City sewers (establishes plumber profession context)
Enemy types as ecological threats emerging from pipes (creature invasion premise)
Wraparound screen mechanic reinforces sewer tunnel environment
Minimal narrative—gameplay and setting communicate the premise

danger_signals: Fireballs floating freely (unpredictable threat, only defeated by kicking after flipping)
Slipice enemies that convert solid platforms to slippery ice (environmental degradation)
Icicles forming on platform undersides in later rounds (escalating environmental hazard)
Fighter Fly that can only be flipped while touching platform (restricted attack window)
environmental_storytelling: Sewer pipes as creature entry points (invasion metaphor)
Multiple interconnected platforms suggesting urban infrastructure
POW block as maintenance tool repurposed for defense
